ddls:
CREATE TABLE "courses" (
  "course_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each course */,
  "course_title" TEXT /* Title of the course */,
  "course_description" TEXT /* Description of the course */,
  "prerequisites" TEXT /* Prerequisites for the course */,
  "credits" REAL /* Number of credits for the course */,
  "grading" TEXT /* Grading scheme for the course (e.g., Letter, Pass/Fail) */,
  "textbook_info" TEXT /* Textbook information for the course */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department offering the course */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the course is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the course was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the course was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("course_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_courses_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instructors" (
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instructor */,
  "instructor_name" TEXT /* Name of the instructor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the instructor */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the instructor belongs to */,
  "office_location" TEXT /* Office location of the instructor */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the instructor */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the instructor is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the instructor record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the instructor record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instructor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instructors_department FOREIGN KEY ("department") REFERENCES departments ("department_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "chair_id" INTEGER /* ID of the chair (instructor) of the department */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the department was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the department was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_chair_id FOREIGN KEY ("chair_id") REFERENCES instructors ("instructor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sections" (
  "section_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each section */,
  "course_id" INTEGER /* ID of the course the section belongs to */,
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instructor teaching the section */,
  "enroll_code" TEXT /* Enrollment code for the section */,
  "session" TEXT /* Session the section is offered in */,
  "days" TEXT /* Days the section is offered */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time the section is offered */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the section */,
  "enrolled" INTEGER /* Number of students enrolled in the section */,
  "max_enrollment" INTEGER /* Maximum enrollment limit for the section */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the section is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the section was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the section was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("section_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sections_course_id FOREIGN KEY ("course_id") REFERENCES courses ("course_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sections_instructor_id FOREIGN KEY ("instructor_id") REFERENCES instructors ("instructor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Name of the student */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the student */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the student */,
  "major" TEXT /* Major of the student */,
  "year_of_study" TEXT /* Year of study of the student (e.g., Freshman, Sophomore) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the student is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the student record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the student record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "enrollments" (
  "enrollment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each enrollment */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student enrolling in the section */,
  "section_id" INTEGER /* ID of the section the student is enrolling in */,
  "enrollment_date" TEXT /* Date the student enrolled in the section */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the enrollment (e.g., Enrolled, Dropped) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the enrollment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the enrollment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("enrollment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_enrollments_section_id FOREIGN KEY ("section_id") REFERENCES sections ("section_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_enrollments_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "course_schedule" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each schedule entry */,
  "course_id" INTEGER /* ID of the course being offered */,
  "term" TEXT /* Term the course is offered in */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the course is offered in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the schedule entry is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the schedule entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the schedule entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_course_schedule_course_id FOREIGN KEY ("course_id") REFERENCES courses ("course_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "waitlists" (
  "waitlist_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each waitlist entry */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student on the waitlist */,
  "section_id" INTEGER /* ID of the section the student is waiting for */,
  "waitlist_date" TEXT /* Date the student was added to the waitlist */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the waitlist entry (e.g., Waiting, Enrolled) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the waitlist entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the waitlist entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("waitlist_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_waitlists_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_waitlists_section_id FOREIGN KEY ("section_id") REFERENCES sections ("section_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grades" (
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade entry */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student receiving the grade */,
  "section_id" INTEGER /* ID of the section the grade is for */,
  "grade" TEXT /* Grade received by the student */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the grade was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the grade was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("grade_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_grades_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_grades_section_id FOREIGN KEY ("section_id") REFERENCES sections ("section_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "recipient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recipient (student or instructor) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message sent to the recipient */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., Enrollment, Assignment) */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_recipient_id FOREIGN KEY ("recipient_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_recipient_id FOREIGN KEY ("recipient_id") REFERENCES instructors ("instructor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "admin_users" (
  "admin_user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each admin user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the admin user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the admin user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the admin user (e.g., admin, staff) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the admin user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the admin user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the admin user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the admin user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the admin user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("admin_user_id")
)
